title: State Machine
description: Each component instance moves through deployment stages: pending, applying, deployed, or failed.
state_pending_desc: Resource has been declared but handler hasn't run yet.
state_deployed_desc: Handler finished successfully. Outputs are available.
state_failed_desc: Handler threw an error. Deployment failed.
deployment_status_body: The overall deployment also tracks a DeploymentStatus with the same values: pending, applying, deployed, failed. This is persisted to Memory and used for crash recovery. If the status is applying on startup, the previous deployment was interrupted.
code_transitions:
Resource states:
  pending → applying → deployed
                    ↘ failed

Deployment lifecycle:
  applying → deployed   (all resources applied successfully)
  applying → failed     (a resource handler threw an error)
removal_body: When a resource is removed from the tree, its cleanup handler runs and it is deleted from the state map entirely. There is no separate "removing" or "removed" state.
persistence_body: The state machine state is part of what gets persisted to Memory. On restart, CReact restores resource states from saved nodes. Nodes with outputs are treated as deployed, others default to pending. The deployment status tells CReact whether the previous run completed or was interrupted mid-apply.
crash_recovery_body: applyingNodeId records which node was being applied when a crash occurred. On restart, canResume() checks if the deployment status is applying, and getInterruptedNodeId() returns that node's ID. The runtime resumes from that node instead of re-applying everything.
audit_log_body: When enableAuditLog is set and the Memory backend supports it, state transitions are recorded as AuditLogEntry records. Actions logged: deploy_start, deploy_complete, deploy_failed, resource_applied, resource_destroyed.
